hard.next_step: 6.29.26 KK - had commercial call. Pricing well received. They understand the timeline is tight and they need to make a decision in the coming week or so. RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday. sharing commercials with value slides on why zycus for platform and partnership offering executive session a Aatish / Bikash 6/22mc- Followed up with Bass Pro to re-engage David and Whitney on the updated proposal. Left David a voicemail and also sent Whitney a text requesting 20–30 minutes with both of them to review the proposal, confirm alignment on scope/pricing, and define next steps. Current risk is increasing as the account has been unresponsive for over a week, despite the proposal being ready for review. Strategy is to use the pending customer reference conversations as a reason to re-engage David and create urgency around the evaluation. If no response is received by Wednesday, June 24, we will escalate thoughtfully by expanding outreach to additional executive stakeholders, including the CFO and CPO/David’s boss, to request an executive alignment meeting. To date, we have held back on broader executive outreach because David indicated the process should move under his direction, but lack of engagement may require us to create additional visibility and momentum to protect the deal .

forecast_read.reason: The win is live (final two, pricing well received 29 Jun) but the 30 Jul date is not: even a selection by 13 Jul leaves under three weeks for an MSA, SOW, InfoSec and signature relay that has not started, and no CFO or CPO has engaged. This is a date and number problem, not a deal problem: re-dated to Sep 2026, Upside Key Deal would be defensible.
forecast_read.math: Days to close: 21 (close 30 Jul 2026, today 9 Jul). Created 12 Mar 2026 (119 days old), qualified 17 Mar. In Shortlisted 43 days (stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted 27 May 2026 per field history). Close date moved twice: 31 Jul to 30 Jun (27 May, Alexa Bradley), then 30 Jun to 30 Jul (29 May, Mario Castro), one net 30-day forward slip. Buyer decision window: David Elford said 29 Jun the decision comes 'in the coming week or so' (6-13 Jul). Pace required for 30 Jul: selection by 13 Jul, then MSA plus SOW plus InfoSec plus signature in 2.5 weeks, versus the normal multi-week relay; implementation itself needs 6-7 months against the Feb 2027 Coupa expiry (27 May call), so a July selection with an Aug-Sep signature is the realistic path.

metrics.narrative: The cost driver is explicit: the evaluation exists because 'our fees with Coupa continued to go up and up and up' and the CFO ordered a market check (David Elford, 30 Mar 2026). Scope numbers are quantified and buyer-provided: $1.4B total spend analyzed, roughly $250M through P2P (David, 6 Apr), 2,000 downstream users, ~225,000 invoices a year, 4,000 expense users and ~$30M T&E spend (Whitney Parks, 21 Apr and 2 Jun emails). What is missing is an agreed value number: ROI slides were walked on 29 Jun and 'pricing well received' (rep log), but no buyer-validated savings or payback figure exists, and David's own test is that the numbers must 'make sure the math makes sense to undertake a conversion' (6 Apr).
sources: Emails 21 Apr and 2 Jun 2026 (Whitney Parks volume data)

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget power sits above the deal team: the CFO initiated the evaluation ('Our CFO has challenged us with looking at what else is out there', David Elford, 30 Mar 2026) and the rep's 22 Jun plan names 'the CFO and CPO/David's boss' as the executive escalation path. Neither is named in Salesforce, on contact roles, or on any call; David has deliberately kept the process 'under his direction' (rep log, 22 Jun). David also confirmed sign-offs beyond him: 'we've got to socialize this with a bunch of other internal folks that would need to sign off before we moved at all' (6 Apr). Role known, person unnamed, zero engagement: the largest single qualification hole 21 days from close.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are buyer-articulated across four read calls: keep today's T&E experience (Egencia booking, mobile receipt capture) with no functionality loss (David, 27 May: 'It's very similar to what we have today. So it's not like we'd be sacrificing any functionality'); one instance across three AS400-era payables ledgers (Infinium, KBM, IDS) plus Workday and the Timberline capital feed (David, 30 Mar and 27 May); control over search and guided buying so preferred content surfaces first (David, 30 Mar: with Coupa 'we have no control over changing that'); their own two-tier taxonomy carried from P2P into spend analytics (Whitney, 19 Mar); responsive support (David, 30 Mar); apples-to-apples modular pricing on a 5-year basis with implementation amortized (David, 6 Apr); and change management for 2,000 downstream users as the deciding anxiety (David, 6 Apr).

decision_process.narrative: The sequence is visible: CFO-initiated market check (30 Mar), a two-vendor evaluation David runs ('this is part of that evaluation, and to be honest, at this point, it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent', 27 May), internal socialization including the accounts payable team ('make sure they're even on board with considering this', 6 Apr), then a decision 'in the coming week or so' (29 Jun rep log) driven by the Feb 2027 Coupa expiry minus a 6-7 month implementation. What is not mapped is the approval chain itself: who signs, in what order, and what the CFO and CPO need to see; David's precondition of a deeper iSource, iContract and iSupplier look (6 Apr) has no completion evidence either.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unstarted and unmapped: no MSA, SOW, redline, InfoSec questionnaire or signature-authority discussion appears in any call, task or rep log through 9 Jul. The only commercial-structure facts are from 6 Apr: professional services run 1.1 to 1.3 times software as a fixed fee with 4-6 milestone payments (Alexa Bradley), and Bass Pro used KPMG for its Coupa implementation but prefers Zycus-run services this time (David, 30 Mar: 'I'd rather use you than a third party'). With a 30 Jul close date, this unbuilt relay is the biggest execution exposure on the record.
sources: SF activities through 7 Jul 2026 (absence of any contracting artifact)

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific, owned, and repeated: Coupa's spend analytics regressed ('instead of being a step forward, it was a step backwards', David, 27 May; 'it's painful to use... most of us aren't using it anymore', Whitney, 19 Mar); search and guided buying surface the wrong content ('that's one of Coupa's biggest weaknesses', David, 30 Mar); support is slow to answer (David, 30 Mar); three separate Coupa instances exist because of three ledgers (David, 30 Mar); intake is fragmented tribal knowledge with no single front door (David, 30 Mar); and fees keep rising under Coupa's private-equity ownership (David, 30 Mar). The CFO's cost challenge converts the pain into a mandate to evaluate.

champion.narrative: David Elford (Director, Non-Merchandise Procurement) is a directive champion: he has anchored six buyer sessions from 12 Mar to 29 Jun, argued internally for the bigger scope ('For 100,000 more, I think it would be foolish of us not to consider going all the way', 6 Apr), pulled new stakeholder Chris into the 27 May session mid-meeting, proposed the 29 Jun commercial review himself (25 Jun email), and welcomed the new rep 'to the evaluation team' (29 Jun email). What developed since the last read: he took the commercial review and set a one-week decision clock. What is missing: he is not the budget owner, has held executive access to himself ('the process should move under his direction', 22 Jun rep log), and has not brokered the CFO or CPO into the room.
sources: SF Next Step 22 Jun and 29 Jun 2026

competition.narrative: A declared two-horse race: 'at this point, it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent. So it's just the two that we're seriously considering' (David Elford, 27 May). Coupa is the six-year incumbent with 2,000 daily users and a contract already extended once to Feb 2027, and it scheduled an onsite for 1 Jul (rep log 29 Jun: 'RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday'), inside the buyer's decision window; its outcome is unknown as of 9 Jul. Zip surfaced early on intake only ('We've had a couple conversations with Zip about intake. The concept is intriguing', David, 30 Mar) and is outside the final two; SAP Concur was waved off on cost for T&E (David, 30 Mar).

critical_signals.text: Coupa, the six-year incumbent with 2,000 daily P2P users, scheduled an onsite at Bass Pro for 1 Jul, inside the buyer's own decision week, and its fallback is a simple renewal since the current contract already runs to February 2027.
The 29 Jun commercial review landed well: pricing was well received and David Elford confirmed Bass Pro must decide within about a week, putting the selection call in the 6 to 13 Jul window.

The proposal was cut 20 percent to 445,200 on corrected volumes and a 5-year term, yet no CFO or CPO has engaged and implementation services (roughly 1.1 to 1.3 times software) still need a budget owner's sign-off.
Reference calls with former Coupa P2P customers, asked for by David Elford on 27 May and promised the same day, remain undelivered while they gate his stated change-management fear for 2,000 users.
Chris, newly hired into the spend and T&E remit after Kory Cooper's departure, was pulled into the 27 May session by David Elford and sat in the 29 Jun commercial review.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Bass Pro has narrowed to Zycus versus incumbent Coupa, pricing was well received on 29 Jun, and the buyer set a decision window of 6 to 13 Jul. The position is strong on champion, pain and criteria but unprotected at the top: no CFO or CPO has engaged, the promised Coupa-replacement references are undelivered, and Coupa took an onsite on 1 Jul while the buyer went quiet. The deal is winnable this month; the 30 Jul paper date is not.

win_position.text: Bass Pro is down to a final two: David Elford said on 27 May, 'it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent. So it's just the two that we're seriously considering.'

The champion is directive: David Elford has anchored six sessions since 12 Mar, pulled Chris into the 27 May meeting, proposed the 29 Jun commercial review himself, and set a one-week decision clock on it.

No economic buyer is engaged 21 days from close: the CFO who launched the evaluation (David, 30 Mar: 'Our CFO has challenged us with looking at what else is out there') and the CPO have never joined a session and are unnamed in Salesforce.
The proof gating selection is open: Coupa-replacement P2P references promised 27 May are undelivered while David's stated nervousness is 'your downstream P2P' (27 May).

deal_momentum.text: Buyer cadence held March through June: technical discovery 19 Mar, 90-minute demo 30 Mar, pricing review 6 Apr, Emburse T&E session 27 May, commercial review 29 Jun, with David proposing the 29 Jun slot himself (25 Jun email).
Commercial topics are live: pricing walked and well received 29 Jun, 5-year term framed, and the buyer acknowledged the tight timeline against the February 2027 Coupa expiry.

No buyer reply logged since 29 Jun despite 30 Jun and 7 Jul follow-ups; the quiet sits inside their declared decision window and the week of Coupa's 1 Jul onsite, so the next few days decide the read.

customer_commitment.text: Whitney Parks picked her sandbox on 29 May ('Zycus for sure, but Emburse wouldn't hurt') and has supplied volume data three times (21 Apr, 29 May, 2 Jun).
David Elford welcomed the new rep 'to the evaluation team' (29 Jun email) and keeps proposing meeting times, including the 29 Jun review slot.
No verbal selection and no mutual close plan: the decision now runs through an internal Bass Pro week Zycus is not in the room for.

deal_risk.text: Incumbent retention push at the decisive moment: Coupa's onsite was set for 1 Jul (logged 29 Jun) and renewal is the zero-effort path, already extended once to Feb 2027.
The 30 Jul close cannot absorb the unstarted MSA, SOW and InfoSec relay even on an immediate win; the realistic signature lands Aug-Sep.

Change management for 2,000 downstream users is the buyer's named 'hard part' (David, 6 Apr) and the AP team is 'nervous about considering anything different' (David, 6 Apr); both stay unmitigated until references land.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer-voiced positives: Whitney Parks on which sandbox she wants (29 May 2026): 'Zycus for sure, but Emburse wouldn't hurt.' David Elford on T&E parity (27 May 2026): 'You've convinced me. It's very similar to what we have today.' Rep-logged buyer reaction to commercials (29 Jun 2026): 'Pricing well received.' Balanced by David (6 Apr 2026): 'I have no doubt. Coupa can serve our business or you can serve our business. The issue is change management.' No buyer statement prefers Zycus over renewing Coupa, so preference is genuine but undeclared.

business_case.evidence: Pricing is framed (three options 6 Apr, revised to 445,200 on 12 Jun on corrected volumes and a 5-year basis; services 1.1 to 1.3 times software as fixed-fee milestones, Alexa Bradley 6 Apr) and ROI slides were walked 29 Jun with 'pricing well received' (rep log). But no buyer-quantified value case exists and no budget owner has validated it; David's test stands: 'make sure the math makes sense to undertake a conversion' (6 Apr 2026).

deal_movement.summary: Five weeks of real movement: stage to Shortlisted and forecast to Upside Key Deal (27 May), proposal repriced to 445,200 (12 Jun), ownership to Karson Keogh (26 Jun), a commercial review with pricing well received and a one-week decision clock (29 Jun), then rep follow-ups into buyer silence (30 Jun, 7 Jul).

items.change: Stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted; forecast moved Pipeline to Best Case to Upside Key Deal (Alexa Bradley)
Close date pulled 31 Jul to 30 Jun (27 May), then re-set to 30 Jul
Amount reduced 556,500 to 445,200 with the updated proposal on corrected volumes and a 5-year basis
Ownership moved Mario Castro to Karson Keogh; division set to New Markets (Alexa Bradley)
Commercial proposal review held: pricing well received, buyer decision due within about a week, Coupa onsite flagged for 1 Jul
Post-holiday re-engagement email to David, Chris and Whitney; no buyer reply logged as of 9 Jul

day_summary.overall: Karson Keogh sent a post-holiday check-in to David Elford, Chris and Whitney Parks, noting Bass Pro ran several evaluation activities the prior week and asking where things stand. It was a rep-side touch; no buyer reply is logged as of 9 Jul. The last buyer engagement remains the 29 Jun proposal review, where pricing was well received and David said a decision is due within about a week.

items.name: Post-holiday check-in to the evaluation team
items.summary: Asked David, Chris and Whitney how last week's evaluation activities went and where the decision stands; awaiting reply.

competitive_position.summary: A declared final two: Zycus versus Coupa, the six-year incumbent (David Elford, 27 May: 'it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent'). Coupa's position is defensive but strong: 2,000 entrenched daily P2P users, a contract already extended once to Feb 2027, zero implementation cost, and a retention onsite scheduled for 1 Jul (logged 29 Jun), landing inside the buyer's 6 to 13 Jul decision window; its outcome is unknown as of 9 Jul. For Bass Pro, doing nothing means renewing Coupa: no retraining, no services spend, but keeping the pains they named on the record (analytics that went 'a step backwards', uncontrollable search, slow support, three separate instances, rising fees under private-equity ownership). Zycus's wedge is those confirmed pains plus Teams-native Merlin Intake, one instance across three ledgers, taxonomy carried from P2P into analytics, and a Feb 2027 timeline that makes July the last safe decision month. The strongest current threat is Coupa's onsite plus buyer silence since 29 Jun: if the onsite bought a renewal concession or a delay, the compressed calendar flips from Zycus's forcing function to Coupa's shield. Zip and SAP Concur were named early, never entered the final two, and are dormant.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday. (rep log, 29 Jun 2026, pointing to 1 Jul). Buyer frustration on record: 'they changed their data visualization engine two, maybe 3 years ago, and instead of being a step forward, it was a step backwards' (David Elford, 27 May 2026); 'our fees with Coupa continued to go up and up and up' (David Elford, 30 Mar 2026).
competitors.source: SF Next Step 29 Jun 2026; Avoma 27 May 2026; Avoma 30 Mar 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver the promised Coupa-replacement P2P references before the decision lands (they answer David's 2,000-user change-management fear in customers' own words), put the CFO and CPO in a Zycus executive session inside the window, and keep the timeline math in front of the buyer: Feb 2027 expiry minus a 6-7 month implementation makes July the last safe selection month, which punishes a renewal-by-inertia. Anchor the value story on their own words: analytics regression, uncontrollable search, three instances collapsed to one, and a 5-year price locked against the fee escalation they resent.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: We've had a couple conversations with Zip about intake. The concept is intriguing. (David Elford, 30 Mar 2026)
competitors.how_we_win: Already outside the final two per David's 27 May down-select; Merlin Intake in Microsoft Teams covers the intake appetite Zip was probing. Monitor only.

competitors.name: SAP Concur
competitors.quote: Concur, I know, is good, but Concur also is expensive... I'd rather stay away from them than somebody that's more economical. (David Elford, 30 Mar 2026)
competitors.how_we_win: Buyer self-eliminated Concur on cost; the Emburse partnership (introduced 27 May, T&E parity confirmed by David) holds this lane.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is aligned on the criteria the buyer can see in a demo (T&E parity, guided buying control, taxonomy, one instance) and exposed exactly where proof requires third parties or hands-on time: downstream P2P confidence, change management for 2,000 users, and the not-yet-run reference calls.

items.criterion: Keep today's T&E experience: Egencia booking retained, mobile-first receipts
items.quote: You've convinced me. It's very similar to what we have today. So it's not like we'd be sacrificing any functionality.
items.source: Avoma 27 May 2026 (David Elford)

items.criterion: One instance across Infinium, KBM and IDS ledgers plus Workday and Timberline capital feed
items.quote: That's probably where my primary concern is (on integrations to 'a lot of antiquated technology').
items.source: Avoma 27 May 2026 (David Elford); iSaaS advance model answer by Ronit

items.criterion: Control over search and guided buying so preferred content surfaces first
items.quote: People go in and type... highlighters, but the first things that come up are not what we would want them to consider at all, and we have no control over changing that.
items.source: Avoma 30 Mar 2026 (David Elford; Ronit confirmed full control)

items.quote: We've got 2,000 people who are used to doing this a certain way that we're going to have to convince that we're going now do it a different way, and that's the hard part.
items.source: Avoma 6 Apr 2026 (David Elford)

items.criterion: Confidence in downstream P2P maturity, proven by peers who left Coupa
items.quote: Probably where I'm most nervous is your downstream P2P... what would be ideal would be if you have customers who use P2P that were previous Coupa customers.
items.source: Avoma 27 May 2026 (David Elford)

items.requirement: Two to three reference calls with P2P customers, ideally former Coupa customers, covering transition, adoption and change management
items.quote: If that's something we could set up would be a couple, I guess, reference calls with folks who use your P2P engine, and what would be ideal would be if you have customers who use P2P that were previous Coupa customers.
items.source: Avoma 27 May 2026; re-confirmed pending in rep log 22 Jun 2026 ('pending customer reference conversations')

items.requirement: Zycus sandbox access for hands-on validation (parked at Whitney's request pending the updated numbers)
items.quote: Zycus for sure, but Emburse wouldn't hurt. (29 May) ... Let's hold on this for now, was thinking the numbers would have been over last week. Once we see those we will regroup. (2 Jun)
items.source: Emails 29 May and 2 Jun 2026 (Whitney Parks)

items.requirement: Updated commercial proposal encompassing Zycus plus Emburse on a 5-year basis
items.quote: But yes, if you could provide us an updated commercial proposal that would encompass you plus [Emburse] that, that would be part of the puzzle as well.
items.source: Avoma 27 May 2026; delivered 10 Jun email 'Zycus Proposal Review'; reviewed 29 Jun ('Pricing well received', rep log)

items.requirement: Preserve Egencia as the booking engine with a mobile-first expense experience
items.quote: Knowing that we could continue to integrate with [Egencia], I think, takes a lot of that concern away.
items.source: Avoma 27 May 2026 (David Elford; Emburse confirmed booking-engine agnostic)

items.requirement: Horizon conference agenda when available, plus access to last year's session recordings
items.quote: Is there an agenda for that yet? When there is, send it over... if we can't go to this year's, getting access to last year's would be almost as good.
items.source: Avoma 27 May 2026 (David Elford, Alexa Bradley)

items.deliverable: Arrange and prep two to three P2P reference calls with downstream Coupa-replacement customers, including question prep both ways
items.grounding_quote: I can work with you on setting up and helping you arrange a couple of p to p specific downstream Coupa replacement customers. We do have them... we'll prep the references. We'll prep you.
items.source: Avoma 27 May 2026 (Alexa Bradley); still pending per rep log 22 Jun 2026; David wanted them inside 'a couple weeks' before his end-June decision (27 May)

items.deliverable: Send the recommended closing plan with dated milestones for the evaluation-to-contract runway

items.source: Avoma 27 May 2026 (Mario Castro); no send evidence through 7 Jul 2026

items.deliverable: Share commercials with value slides (why Zycus, platform and partnership) and deliver the offered executive session with Aatish / Bikash
items.grounding_quote: sharing commercials with value slides on why zycus for platform and partnership offering executive session a Aatish / Bikash
items.source: SF Next Step 29 Jun 2026 (Karson Keogh)

items.deliverable: Send Horizon materials: this year's agenda when published and the link to last year's session recordings

items.deliverable: Make the vendor decision within the stated window (about 6 to 13 Jul, derived from 'the coming week or so' on 29 Jun)

items.deliverable: Regroup on the sandbox now that the updated numbers were delivered (10 Jun) and reviewed (29 Jun)

items.area: Contact-role hygiene: Chris (in the 27 May and 29 Jun meetings) and the CFO/CPO are known to exist but absent from the opportunity's contact roles
items.quote: Hi David, Chris, and Whitney, Great meeting you today.
items.source: Email 29 Jun 2026; OpportunityContactRole list

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is one internal Bass Pro week from a decision. The levers that swing it are executive coverage above the champion, third-party proof for the change-management fear, and making the Feb 2027 timeline work for Zycus instead of for the incumbent.
flags: Multi-thread above David before the decision lands: the CFO started this evaluation and the CPO is David's boss, yet neither has met Zycus; convert the executive session already offered on 29 Jun (Aatish / Bikash) into a booked CFO/CPO meeting inside the window.

Beat the incumbent's 1 Jul onsite with proof, not slides: the two Coupa-replacement P2P references promised on 27 May directly answer David's 2,000-user change-management fear and are the only outstanding buyer-requested proof point.

Turn the calendar into the wedge: Feb 2027 expiry minus a 6-7 month implementation makes July the last safe selection month; deliver the promised closing plan with dated milestones so a slip becomes visibly Coupa's risk, not Zycus's.

items.name: David Elford
items.title: Director, Non-Merchandise Procurement
items.sentiment: Positive and directive: ran six sessions, argued for the bigger scope (6 Apr), confirmed pricing well received and a one-week decision (29 Jun).
items.risk: Runs the decision but does not own the budget; has kept executive access to himself ('the process should move under his direction', 22 Jun rep log) and has been silent since 29 Jun, through Coupa's 1 Jul onsite.
items.source: OpportunityContactRole; Avoma 12 Mar, 30 Mar, 6 Apr, 27 May 2026; emails 25 Jun and 29 Jun 2026; SF Next Step 22 and 29 Jun 2026

items.name: Whitney Parks
items.title: Non Merchandise Procurement Manager

items.risk: Carrying extra load after 'a few key departures' (her email, 21 Apr); her sandbox ask has sat parked since 2 Jun, an open thread that keeps her advocacy warm if re-opened.
items.source: OpportunityContactRole; Avoma 19 Mar, 30 Mar, 6 Apr, 27 May 2026; emails 21 Apr to 25 Jun 2026

items.risk: Recently hired into the spend/T&E remit after Kory Cooper's departure; surname taken from the 25 Jun email cc and he is not yet a Salesforce contact role, so his exact title and influence are unverified.

champion_strength.summary: David Elford is a strong, directive champion: he has anchored every buyer session from 12 Mar to 29 Jun, pulled new stakeholders in mid-meeting (Chris, 27 May), argued internally for the larger scope (6 Apr), proposed the 29 Jun commercial review himself, and set the one-week decision clock. The single flaw is unchanged since the last sweep: he is not the budget owner and has kept the CFO and CPO out of the room ('the process should move under his direction', 22 Jun rep log), so his advocacy cannot sign the contract. His silence since 29 Jun reads as decision-week process cadence, not disengagement, but it coincides with Coupa's 1 Jul onsite and should break by 14 Jul.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole; Avoma 12 Mar, 19 Mar (referenced), 30 Mar, 6 Apr, 27 May 2026; emails 25 Jun and 29 Jun 2026; SF Next Step 22 and 29 Jun 2026

expansion_context.note: New-logo displacement of incumbent Coupa; no sibling Closed-Won evidence in this read. Bass Pro last evaluated Zycus roughly 8 years ago when it selected Coupa (David Elford, 30 Mar 2026: 'we looked at you all at the time we selected Coupa, but it seemed like your p two p was fairly immature 8 years ago').

scope_change.from: Full S2P option discussed 6 Apr 2026 at roughly 556,500 a year list (David Elford: 'we'd go all the way to the right to the full platform'), including iSource, iContract and iSupplier
scope_change.to: Downstream-plus-analytics scope at 445,200 (12 Jun 2026): eProcurement, eInvoicing, ML iAnalyze, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, iSaaS, with Emburse as the T&E partner
scope_change.detail: The 12 Jun repricing followed buyer feedback and corrected assumptions (P2P spend roughly $250M, not the $800M assumed; iSave and iManage removed on 6 Apr). The Salesforce product list no longer names the upstream modules David leaned toward on 6 Apr; a phased path was his own earlier preference (30 Mar: 'Could we do p two p and spin analytics and then come back at a later point and do sourcing and contracts?'). Whether upstream is deferred or dropped is unconfirmed (inference from the amount change plus the product list).

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is central to the pitch and landed as a differentiator, not a gimmick: Merlin Intake inside Microsoft Teams anchored the 30 Mar demo ('your whole intake capability is intriguing as well', David Elford), AP Smartdesk AI extraction read as comparable-plus to today ('this seems pretty comparable to what we're doing today', David to Kory Cooper), autonomous negotiation for tail spend drew 'as common sense solution as I've seen' (David), and Merlin Insight natural-language spend analysis got 'the concept is intriguing' with an accuracy caveat. Agentic AI and Merlin are in the priced scope. Positioning is strong; the buyer wants proof in practice, not more AI story.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Bass Pro is interested but show-me skeptical: David Elford (30 Mar 2026): 'a lot of these AI things sound cool, but then when they start actually running, what you get out of them is not real helpful, but the concept is intriguing.' They engaged seriously with Merlin Intake, AI guided buying and AI invoice extraction, and accepted AI-heavy scope (Merlin, Agentic AI) in the proposal, but no AI mandate or governance program of their own surfaced.

items.detail: No engaged budget owner 21 days from close: the CFO who initiated the evaluation (David Elford, 30 Mar: 'Our CFO has challenged us with looking at what else is out there') and the CPO (David's boss) have never met Zycus and are unnamed in Salesforce; David has kept the process 'under his direction' (22 Jun rep log). A 445,200 software plus roughly 490,000 to 580,000 services decision has no executive sponsor on the buyer side.
items.source: Avoma 30 Mar 2026; SF Next Step 22 Jun 2026

items.detail: Buyer-requested proof still undelivered: David asked for two to three references on 6 Apr and specified former-Coupa P2P customers on 27 May; Alexa Bradley committed to arrange and prep them the same day; the 22 Jun rep log still calls them 'pending customer reference conversations.' They gate the change-management concern that decides this deal.
items.source: Avoma 6 Apr and 27 May 2026; SF Next Step 22 Jun 2026

items.detail: The buyer's named deciding fear: 'we've got 2,000 people who are used to doing this a certain way... that's the hard part' (David Elford, 6 Apr), and the AP team 'they know they're nervous about considering anything different' (David, 6 Apr). Coupa renewal requires none of this effort, which is exactly the incumbent's pitch.
items.source: Avoma 6 Apr 2026 (David Elford)

items.detail: Selection must land in July for the deal to work: implementation runs 6-7 months (Mario Castro, 27 May slide; David: 'If we were to try to switch, we got to get going soon') against the Feb 2027 Coupa expiry, and the buyer set a decision window of 6 to 13 Jul on 29 Jun. The 30 Jul close date cannot also absorb contracting; a decision delay past mid-July starts to favor renewal by default.
items.source: Avoma 27 May 2026; SF Next Step 29 Jun 2026

items.detail: Incumbent retention push at the decisive moment: Coupa onsite scheduled for 1 Jul (logged 29 Jun: 'RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday'), followed by buyer silence through 9 Jul despite 30 Jun and 7 Jul follow-ups. Outcome unknown; if Coupa bought a delay or a renewal concession, the compressed calendar flips against Zycus.
items.source: SF Next Step 29 Jun 2026; emails 30 Jun and 7 Jul 2026 unanswered

items.detail: Buyer-side churn inside the evaluating team: Kory Cooper (ran the Coupa environment, active in the 19 Mar and 30 Mar sessions) left Bass Pro around 3 Apr (David, 6 Apr: 'Corey from our team, last Friday was his last day'); Whitney flagged 'a few key departures that has me a bit behind' (21 Apr). Replacement voice Chris joined 27 May and 29 Jun, partially rebuilding the thread, but his role is unverified in Salesforce.
items.source: Avoma 6 Apr 2026; email 21 Apr 2026 (Whitney Parks); Avoma 27 May 2026

items.detail: David's stated 'primary concern' technically (27 May): integrations to 'really old IBM AS400 technology' payables ledgers (Infinium, KBM, IDS) plus the Timberline (Sage) capital system and Workday. Ronit proposed the iSaaS advance model (Zycus carries ~80 percent) and offered an integration-expert session, even onsite (30 Mar); no such deep-dive is logged since.
items.source: Avoma 30 Mar and 27 May 2026

confidence_signals.summary: Two-way engagement held through 29 Jun (buyer proposed the meeting time, attended, and reacted well to pricing); since then three rep touches (29 Jun, 30 Jun, 7 Jul) have gone unanswered. The silence sits inside the buyer's own declared decision week plus the July 4 holiday, so it reads as process cadence rather than cooling, but it expires: no buyer response by about 14 Jul means the window slipped silently and the read flips.
items: Buyer-proposed meeting: David Elford offered 'Monday (June 29th) at 3:00 pm Central?' himself (email, 25 Jun 2026).
Positive commercial reaction: 'Pricing well received' and a self-set one-week decision clock (rep log, 29 Jun 2026).
Watch item: no buyer reply to the 30 Jun and 7 Jul emails as of 9 Jul, across Coupa's 1 Jul onsite week.

ceo_intervention.reason: The deal is in a final-two against the incumbent with the decision landing 6 to 13 Jul, and the deal team has already put a CEO-level session on the table (rep log, 29 Jun: 'offering executive session a Aatish / Bikash'). The buyer's evaluation was initiated by its CFO, who has never met Zycus; the only Zycus executive engaged (Alexa Bradley, VP Sales) has been in the deal since April without producing CFO/CPO engagement. A founder-CEO conversation about partnership and multi-year commitment is the one move Coupa's renewal pitch cannot match in the days that remain.
ceo_intervention.ceo_action: Deliver the already-offered executive session as a CEO-to-CFO/CPO conversation before the decision lands: a personal partnership commitment (executive attention through implementation, price protection over the 5-year term) that directly answers Bass Pro's rising-fee and change-management fears.

buyer_target.title: CFO (evaluation sponsor) or CPO, David Elford's boss; neither is named in Salesforce

ceo_intervention.why_not_vp: Alexa Bradley (VP Sales, North America) has been executive sponsor on calls since 6 Apr and sent the 15 Jun executive continuity email, yet no CFO/CPO engagement resulted; the deal team itself escalated past VP level by offering the Aatish / Bikash session on 29 Jun. The buyer-side peer for the closing conversation is the CFO who launched the evaluation, above a VP-to-director thread.
lower_execs_engaged: Alexa Bradley (VP of Sales, North America): calls 6 Apr and 27 May 2026, executive continuity email 15 Jun 2026

items.action: Deliver the two promised Coupa-replacement P2P reference calls to David Elford before his vendor decision lands.
items.trigger: Buyer decision due 'in the coming week or so' (29 Jun log) while the references promised 27 May are still pending per the 22 Jun log.
items.expected_effect: Answers David's stated change-management and downstream-P2P fears in peers' own words during the exact week Bass Pro chooses between Zycus and a Coupa renewal.

items.action: Convert the offered executive session into a booked meeting with Bass Pro's CFO and CPO this week.
items.trigger: The 29 Jun log offers an 'executive session a Aatish / Bikash' while no budget owner has ever met Zycus and Coupa went onsite 1 Jul.
items.expected_effect: Puts the budget approver in the room before the decision is made without Zycus present; counters whatever Coupa's onsite promised with a partnership commitment only Zycus leadership can make.

items.action: Send David the mutual close and implementation plan working back from the February 2027 Coupa expiry.
items.trigger: Closing plan promised 27 May ('a closing plan... certain milestone that we recommend to hit') with no send evidence; buyer acknowledged the tight timeline 29 Jun.
items.expected_effect: Makes July selection the buyer's own logical conclusion (6-7 month implementation math) and turns any slip into a visible risk to Bass Pro's Feb 2027 cutover, not just to Zycus's quarter.

items.action: Pre-stage the MSA, SOW skeleton and InfoSec pack so contracting starts the day Bass Pro selects.
items.trigger: Paper process is a gap: no MSA, SOW, or security artifact exists on the record while the close date allows 2.5 weeks post-decision.
items.expected_effect: Cuts weeks off the selection-to-signature relay, protects an Aug-Sep signature against the Feb 2027 go-live math, and signals execution readiness versus the incumbent's inertia pitch.

items.action: Re-open Whitney's parked sandbox and confirm whether the upstream module review still gates final sign-off.
items.trigger: Whitney parked the sandbox 2 Jun pending the updated numbers, which were delivered 10 Jun and reviewed 29 Jun; David's 6 Apr module-review precondition has no completion evidence.
items.expected_effect: Gives David the hands-on P2P proof he said he needs ('it's hard to really fully understand how a system works till you get in there'), deepens Whitney and Chris as advocates, and closes the last stated precondition to selection.

calls_omitted.call: Zycus X Bass pro Intro Call (2026-03-12)
calls_omitted.reason: No transcript or notes in the evidence package; call existence confirmed by the SF Event.

calls_omitted.call: Proposal Review with Zycus - Bass Pro (2026-06-29)
calls_omitted.reason: Meeting exists (SF Event plus Avoma listing) but carries no transcript or notes; content taken only from the rep's dated Next Step log and the 29-30 Jun follow-up emails, so the read of the decisive commercial meeting is partial.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities last 120d (through 2026-07-07), field history 12 Mar to 29 Jun 2026, contact roles, Next_Step__c current as of 29 Jun 2026
gaps: 29 Jun 2026 commercial review not deep-read (no transcript or notes); summarized from the rep log and emails only.
12 Mar 2026 intro call not read (no transcript or notes).
Outcome of Coupa's 1 Jul 2026 onsite unknown; buyer silent 30 Jun through 9 Jul.
No named CFO or CPO anywhere in Salesforce or the calls; economic-buyer identity and approval chain unconfirmed.
